what: v0.2.0 — Quarto scaffolding and execution context detection

text: Three new functions added. detect_execution_context() identifies three environments code currently running : interactive R session, quarto render call, plain Rscript invocation. Returns one "interactive", "quarto", "rscript". useful writing code resolves input file paths correctly across three contexts – persistent pain point researchers run script interactively, part Quarto document, HPC cluster. create_qmd() scaffolds new Quarto document reproducible template. what: Decision: arborize() as a toolero function
title: toolero – Development Journal

title: toolero – Development Journal
text: Two Typst packages exist linguistic trees: @preview/syntree:0.2.1 (simple notation) – takes bracket notation string. Compact, familiar linguists, simpler interface. Version 0.2.0 broken Typst 0.12+ due style() function removed. Version 0.2.1 fixes . @preview/lingotree:1.0.0 (structured notation) – takes nested tree() function calls. powerful: supports per-node styling, movement arrows, multi-dominant trees. Released November 2025, compatible current Typst. tree_notation argument controls backend used: - "simple" – bracket notation string, uses syntree 0.2.1 - "structured" – nested tree() calls string, uses lingotree 1.0.0 user provides complete tree string appropriate format. arborize() wraps correct Typst scaffolding.

title: toolero – Development Journal
text: lingotree backend require arborize() parse bracket notation convert nested tree() calls. user responsible providing correct string format. keeps arborize() simple.

title: toolero – Development Journal
text: arborize() performs six steps: 1. Validates inputs resolves Typst package tree_notation 2. Builds minimal .qmd document via .build_arborize_qmd() 3. Writes document inside withr::with_tempdir() automatic cleanup 4. quarto::quarto_render() produces intermediate PDF via Typst 5. pdftools::pdf_convert() converts PDF PNG 6. PNG bytes read memory temp dir deleted, written output path 7. provenance = TRUE, .write_arborize_provenance() writes .yaml file

title: toolero – Development Journal
text: provenance = TRUE (default) writes companion .yaml file alongside PNG recording: tree string, tree_notation, typst_package, dpi, papersize, margin, rendered_by, rendered_at, output path. makes renders reproducible modifiable without hunting original tree string. parameter name provenance chosen save_source, keep_source, write_source meaningful name context – records provenance image, matching DATA-PROVENANCE.md convention established elsewhere toolero. rearborize() function considered rejected v0.4.0. re-render pattern (read yaml, call arborize() recovered arguments) five lines justify exported function. Deferred pending evidence demand.

title: toolero – Development Journal
text: papersize margin arguments determine tightly PNG cropped around tree. key insight: papersize match tree’s complexity, default blindly "a5". Practical guidance: - "a6" "a7" simple trees (2-4 nodes) - "a5" medium trees (default) - "a4" wide deep trees - "a3" wide trees margin provides buffer around tree. Default "0.5cm" works cases. guidance documented arborize() @details vignette.

title: toolero – Development Journal

title: toolero – Development Journal
text: Simple notation (syntree): tree string goes inside Typst string literal delimited double quotes. Double quotes inside string must escaped. gsub() call: gsub('"', '\\\\"', tree, fixed = TRUE). match escaped result tests: grepl('\\\\"cat\\\\"', result, fixed = TRUE). Four backslashes R pattern = two literal backslashes string. Structured notation (lingotree): tree string goes directly Typst code, inside string literal. escaping needed. User bears syntax responsibility. General principle: user data crosses quoted string literal another language, escaping required. inserted raw code, escaping user bears syntax responsibility.

code: library(toolero)  project_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "my-analysis")  # 1. Create a project with sensible defaults init_project(path = project_dir)  # 2. Audit the project structure check_project(path = project_dir)  # 3. Scaffold a reproducible Quarto analysis document create_qmd(path = project_dir, filename = "analysis.qmd")  # 4. Write the cleaned data write_clean_csv(data, file.path(project_dir, "data", "clean.csv"))  # 7. Apply an analysis function to each subset and collect the results results <- run_by_group(   manifest = file.path(project_dir, "data", "jobs", "manifest.csv"),   .f       = my_analysis )

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: Scaffolds new Quarto document reproducible template optional sample data, custom styling, YAML pre-population, post-render hook automatically extracts R code rendered document companion .R file. function two main motivations. First, reduces repetitive setup work. regularly create Quarto documents author information, institutional metadata, preferred format settings, yaml_data argument lets pre-populate YAML header personal configuration file instead rebuilding header hand. Second, helps reduce code drift. literate programming workflow, .qmd document can serve source truth: prose, code, results, interpretation live together. post-render hook derives standalone .R script document automatically, maintain separate script hand. pattern discussed detail post Notebook Cluster. Part 1: Start Document. Arguments: filename – name .qmd file. Must supplied explicitly. path – directory document created. Defaults ".". yaml_data – path YAML file pre-populating header. overwrite – whether overwrite existing files. Defaults FALSE. use_purl – TRUE (default), scaffolds _quarto.yml R/purl.R. include_examples – TRUE (default), copies sample dataset data-raw/, placeholder logo assets/, uses worked example template. FALSE, creates blank skeleton. use_style – controls custom styling. FALSE (default) produces plain Quarto output. TRUE scans assets/ .css .html files wires YAML. directory path scans directory instead.
code: # Blank skeleton -- no examples, no styling, no purl hook create_qmd(path = "my-project", filename = "analysis.qmd",            include_examples = FALSE, use_purl = FALSE)  # Full worked example with sample data and placeholder logo (default) create_qmd(path = "my-project", filename = "analysis.qmd")  # Blank document wired to branding assets in assets/ create_qmd(path = "my-project", filename = "report.qmd",            include_examples = FALSE, use_style = TRUE)  # Blank document with custom branding from another directory create_qmd(path = "my-project", filename = "report.qmd",            include_examples = FALSE, use_style = "my-branding/")  # Pre-populate YAML from a personal config file create_qmd(path = "my-project", filename = "analysis.qmd",            yaml_data = "my-config.yml")

title: A Toolkit for Research Workflows
text: two functions form split-apply pair heart toolero’s workflow support. idea simple: split data , apply analysis function piece collect results. split apply deliberately separate steps can iterate analysis function without re-splitting data time. write_by_group() handles split. partitions data frame grouping column, writes one CSV per group sanitized filenames, optionally produces manifest.csv records group’s name, file path, row count. manifest input run_by_group(). run_by_group() handles apply. reads subset manifest, calls function one, assembles results single tibble. function returns data frame, output automatically unnested flat tibble group ID column prepended. returns anything else — model, plot, file path — results come back nested tibble list-column. analyses slow computationally independent across groups, run_by_group() supports parallel execution via furrr. workers argument controls many R sessions use. ceiling parallel::detectCores(logical = FALSE) - 1L, reserves one core main session. manifest produced write_by_group() also input submitr::htc_gen_submit() multiple-job mode, making split-apply pattern natural -ramp high-throughput computing local parallelism enough.
code: sample_path <- system.file("templates", "sample.csv", package = "toolero") penguins    <- read_clean_csv(sample_path)  # Split to disk write_by_group(   penguins,   group_col  = "species",   output_dir = "data/jobs",   manifest   = TRUE )  # Define an analysis function summarise_species <- function(data) {   dplyr::summarise(data,     n            = dplyr::n(),     mean_mass    = mean(body_mass_g, na.rm = TRUE),     mean_flipper = mean(flipper_length_mm, na.rm = TRUE)   ) }  # Apply from disk via manifest -- returns a flat tibble results <- run_by_group(   manifest = "data/jobs/manifest.csv",   .f       = summarise_species )  # Apply from memory via named list -- same result, no disk reads subsets <- split(penguins, penguins$species)  results <- run_by_group(   groups = subsets,   .f     = summarise_species ) # Parallel execution across 3 workers results <- run_by_group(   manifest = "data/jobs/manifest.csv",   .f       = summarise_species,   workers  = 3L )

what: Render a syntactic tree as a PNG image — arborize
title: Render a syntactic tree as a PNG image — arborize
text: Takes syntactic tree renders using Quarto's Typst engine, exporting result PNG image. Supports two rendering backends controlled tree_notation:

title: Render a syntactic tree as a PNG image — arborize
code: arborize(   tree,   output = "syntactic-tree.png",   dpi = 300,   tree_notation = c("simple", "structured"),   papersize = "a5",   margin = "0.5cm",   provenance = TRUE,   overwrite = FALSE )

title: Render a syntactic tree as a PNG image — arborize
text: tree character string. tree_notation = "simple", bracket notation string e.g. "[S [NP] [VP]]". tree_notation = "structured", lingotree tree() call string. output character string. Path output PNG file. Defaults "syntactic-tree.png" current working directory. dpi numeric value. Resolution output PNG dots per inch. Defaults 300. Use 600 print-quality output. tree_notation character string. One "simple" (default) "structured". Controls Typst rendering backend used. See Details. papersize character string. Typst paper size intermediate PDF. Defaults "a5". Increase "a4" wide trees. margin character string. Page margin intermediate PDF. Defaults "0.5cm". Reduce tighter crops around tree. provenance logical. Whether write companion .yaml file recording tree string rendering arguments alongside PNG. Defaults TRUE. provenance file name PNG .yaml extension lives directory. Pass FALSE suppress . overwrite logical. Whether overwrite existing output files. TRUE, overwrites PNG provenance file exist. Defaults FALSE.

title: Render a syntactic tree as a PNG image — arborize
text: Invisibly returns path output PNG file.

title: Render a syntactic tree as a PNG image — arborize
text: "simple" uses @preview/syntree accepts bracket notation string, e.g. "[S [NP [Det ] [N cat]] [VP [V sat]]]". compact input format suits basic linguistic trees. "structured" uses @preview/lingotree accepts nested tree() call string. backend supports per-node styling, movement arrows, multi-dominant trees. function useful producing standalone tree figures can embedded document format – LaTeX, Word, HTML, presentations – without requiring full LaTeX installation. arborize() performs following steps: Validates inputs resolves Typst package tree_notation. Builds minimal .qmd document via .build_arborize_qmd(). Writes document renders inside self-cleaning temporary directory managed withr::with_tempdir(). Calls quarto::quarto_render() produce intermediate PDF via Typst. Converts PDF PNG using pdftools::pdf_convert(). Reads PNG bytes memory temporary directory deleted, writes specified output path. provenance = TRUE, writes companion .yaml file recording tree string rendering arguments. first use, Typst download required package Typst package registry. requires internet connection. Subsequent renders use locally cached package. Requires Quarto 1.4 later Typst support, pdftools package PDF--PNG conversion. Install pdftools install.packages("pdftools").

title: Render a syntactic tree as a PNG image — arborize
text: syntree Typst package (v0.2.1): https://typst.app/universe/package/syntree lingotree Typst package (v1.0.0): https://typst.app/universe/package/lingotree

title: Render a syntactic tree as a PNG image — arborize
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Simple bracket notation (default) -- also writes tree-1.yaml arborize("[NP [Det the] [N cat]]", output = "my-trees/tree-1.png")  # Suppress provenance file arborize("[NP [Det the] [N cat]]", provenance = FALSE)  # Wider tree with print-quality output arborize(   paste0(     "[Aspectual Classes ",     "[Statives [States]] ",     "[Dynamic ",     "[Atelic [Activities]] ",     "[Telic ",     "[Instantaneous [Achievements]] ",     "[Durative [Accomplishments]]]]]"   ),   output    = "aspectual-classes.png",   dpi       = 600,   papersize = "a4" )  # Structured notation using lingotree arborize(   "tree(     tag: [VP],     tree(       tag: [DP],       [every],       [farmer]     ),     [smiled]   )",   tree_notation = "structured",   output        = "vp-tree.png" ) } # }

text: filename string NULL. Name generated .qmd file. Must supplied explicitly, e.g. "analysis.qmd". path string. Path directory document created. Defaults "." (current working directory). yaml_data string NULL. Path YAML file containing metadata pre-populate document header. NULL (default), template copied -placeholder prompts intact. overwrite logical. title: Generate a KB-importable XML file from a Quarto document — generate_kb_xml
text: generate_kb_xml() performs following steps: Validates html_path exists. Infers qmd_path html_path supplied, validates . Extracts title, description, categories .qmd YAML header maps kb_title, kb_summary, kb_keywords. Re-renders .qmd isolated temporary directory embed-resources: true CSS, images, JS self-contained. data/ assets/ folders copied alongside .qmd ensure render succeeds. Extracts <body> embedded HTML. Escapes HTML entities body XML compatibility, required UW-Madison KB import format. Builds XML structure kb_title, kb_keywords, kb_summary, kb_body nodes. Writes .xml file output_dir. Temporary files managed via withr::local_tempdir() automatically cleaned function exits, even error. importing resulting XML KB, check Decode HTML entity body content option.

title: Apply a function to each group in a manifest or named list — run_by_group
text: run_by_group() apply half split-apply workflow toolero. split half write_by_group(), partitions data frame grouping column writes one file per group along manifest.   split done . apply step can run many times iterate analysis function.
code: # Split to disk write_by_group(penguins, group_col = "species",                output_dir = "data/jobs", manifest = TRUE)  # Apply from disk via manifest results <- run_by_group(   manifest = "data/jobs/manifest.csv",   .f       = my_analysis )  # Apply from memory via named list subsets <- penguins |>   dplyr::group_split(species) |>   setNames(c("Adelie", "Chinstrap", "Gentoo"))  results <- run_by_group(   groups = subsets,   .f     = my_analysis )

title: Apply a function to each group in a manifest or named list — run_by_group
text: .f receives single data frame first argument. can return anything, return type must consistent across groups. Consistency evaluated bucket: either groups return data frame (tabular) none (non-tabular). Mixed returns cause error identifying groups returned unexpected types. Common return types output shape: one-row tibble summary statistics – unnested flat table multi-row tibble (e.g. model coefficients) – unnested group ID repeated per row model object – returned list-column ggplot object – returned list-column file path – returned list-column

title: Apply a function to each group in a manifest or named list — run_by_group
code: # \donttest{ sample_path <- system.file("templates", "sample.csv", package = "toolero") penguins <- read_clean_csv(sample_path)  # Split the data to disk tmp <- tempdir() write_by_group(penguins, group_col = "species",                output_dir = tmp, manifest = TRUE) #> ✔ Written "Adelie" (152 rows) to /tmp/RtmpoO4xBn/adelie.csv #> ✔ Written "Chinstrap" (68 rows) to /tmp/RtmpoO4xBn/chinstrap.csv #> ✔ Written "Gentoo" (124 rows) to /tmp/RtmpoO4xBn/gentoo.csv #> ✔ Manifest written to /tmp/RtmpoO4xBn/manifest.csv  # Define an analysis function summarise_species <- function(data) {   dplyr::summarise(data,     n            = dplyr::n(),     mean_mass    = mean(body_mass_g, na.rm = TRUE),     mean_flipper = mean(flipper_length_mm, na.rm = TRUE)   ) }  # Apply via manifest -- returns a flat tibble results <- run_by_group(   manifest = file.path(tmp, "manifest.csv"),   .f       = summarise_species )  # Apply via named list in memory subsets <- penguins |>   dplyr::group_split(species) |>   setNames(c("Adelie", "Chinstrap", "Gentoo"))  results <- run_by_group(   groups = subsets,   .f     = summarise_species )  # Apply a function that returns a model -- returns a nested tibble fit_model <- function(data) {   lm(body_mass_g ~ flipper_length_mm, data = data) }  models <- run_by_group(   manifest = file.path(tmp, "manifest.csv"),   .f       = fit_model )  # Parallel execution across 3 workers results <- run_by_group(   manifest = file.path(tmp, "manifest.csv"),   .f       = summarise_species,   workers  = 3L )  # Parallel execution across 3 workers results <- run_by_group(   manifest = file.path(tmp, "manifest.csv"),   .f       = summarise_species,   workers  = 3L )  # Reproducible parallel execution with a fixed seed random_summary <- function(data) {   tibble::tibble(val = sample(seq_len(nrow(data)), 1)) }  results <- run_by_group(   manifest = file.path(tmp, "manifest.csv"),   .f       = random_summary,   workers  = 2L,   seed     = 1234 ) # }

what: toolero: A Toolkit for Research Workflows — toolero-package
title: toolero: A Toolkit for Research Workflows — toolero-package
text: Provides utility functions help researchers implement best practices coding projects. Includes tools reading cleaning data files, initializing R projects standard folder structure, creating 'Quarto' documents reproducible templates optional sample data custom styling, detecting execution context across interactive, 'Quarto', script-based workflows, splitting data frames group-level output files, rendering syntactic tree diagrams standalone PNG images via 'Typst'.

title: toolero 0.4.0
text: Added run_by_group(), apply half split-apply workflow. Accepts either manifest CSV produced write_by_group(manifest = TRUE) named list data frames. Applies user-supplied function group subset collects results flat tibble (function returns data frame) nested tibble list-column (returns anything else). Supports parallel execution via furrr future workers argument, hard ceiling parallel::detectCores(logical = FALSE) - 1L reserve one core main session. seed argument enables reproducible parallel execution analyses involving randomness. Added read_clean_csv() reading CSV files tibble janitor::clean_names() applied automatically. Supports explicit missing-value codes via na, selective row dropping via drop_na (accepts TRUE character vector column names), optional ingest summary via summary, pass-arguments readr::read_csv() via .... Added write_clean_csv() writing data frames CSV clean column names. Applies janitor::clean_names() column names already clean reports affected columns via cli feedback. Added check_project() auditing project directory toolero conventions. Checks expected folders, .Rproj file, renv.lock, git repository, README, .gitignore, hidden files .RData .Rhistory. Operates two modes: cli report (default) tibble return programmatic use (error = FALSE). Added qmd_to_r() extracting R code chunks .qmd file standalone .R script via knitr::purl(). output path defaults directory input extension replaced. documentation argument controls much context preserved extracted script. Added Palmer Penguins attribution (Horst, Hill & Gorman, 2020) template .qmd, create_qmd() roxygen @details section, provenance note inst/extdata/.

title: toolero 0.4.0
text: create_qmd(): longer copies styles.css header.html package project. Custom styling now controlled exclusively new use_style argument. Projects relied create_qmd() copying UW-branded assets use init_project(uw_branding = TRUE) scaffold files, pass use_style = TRUE create_qmd() wire YAML. create_qmd(): sample data now copied data-raw/ instead data/, consistent init_project()’s folder structure.

title: toolero 0.4.0
text: create_qmd(): added include_examples argument (default TRUE). TRUE, copies sample dataset (sample.csv) data-raw/, placeholder logo (logo.png) assets/, uses worked example template params block referencing sample data. FALSE, creates blank skeleton .qmd YAML header setup chunk – sample data, logo, example analysis block. create_qmd(): added use_style argument (default FALSE). Accepts FALSE (custom styling), TRUE (scans assets/ .css .html files), directory path (scans directory instead). exactly one .css file found, added css: YAML. exactly one .html file found, added include--body:. multiple files either type found, function errors asks user specify one use via yaml_data. decouples styling init_project() supports non-UW branding workflows. Added inst/templates/skeleton.qmd – minimal Quarto template used include_examples = FALSE. Contains YAML header, setup chunk library(toolero), single placeholder heading. Added inst/templates/logo.png – placeholder logo image copied assets/ include_examples = TRUE. Reads “logo goes ” user knows replace branding.

title: toolero 0.3.0
text: create_qmd(): filename now first argument default – must supplied explicitly. path now second argument defaults ".", allowing natural calls like create_qmd("analysis.qmd"). write_by_group(): sanitized output filenames now use - (dash) separator instead _ (underscore), consistent package convention file names use dashes. Existing workflows reference output paths name need update accordingly. init_project(): file_path argument renamed path consistency create_qmd() broader package API. Calls using file_path = name error; positional calls unaffected.

Extracts metadata .qmd YAML header re-renders embedded resources self-contained import. create_qmd(): added use_purl argument (default TRUE) scaffolds _quarto.yml post-render hook purl.R script extracting R code rendered documents R/.

text: init_project(): now runs renv::snapshot() creates .renvignore renv::init(), ensuring lockfile populated .qmd files excluded dependency scanning project creation time. create_qmd(): _quarto.yml now copied inst/templates/ rather written hardcoded string, changes template reflected automatically. create_qmd(): purl.R now correctly placed R/ instead project root, consistent _quarto.yml calling Rscript R/purl.R. create_qmd(): fixed YAML boolean serialization yaml_data supplied. yaml::.yaml() converting true/false yes/, Quarto recognize. custom handler now forces unquoted true/false output. inst/templates/purl.R: replaced QUARTO_DOCUMENT_PATH environment variable approach fs::dir_ls() glob scan, works reliably regardless Quarto invokes post-render script.
